summary: NumPy pytest execution crashed with a fatal segmentation fault during test_datetime.py execution, after successfully passing test__exceptions.py. The build completed despite warnings about missing headers in CMake configuration checks.
root_cause: Runtime segmentation fault (Fatal Python error: Segmentation fault) in NumPy C extension code during pytest execution, specifically when running test_datetime.py

evidence.reason: Direct evidence that pytest crashed with a segmentation fault rather than a test assertion failure or normal error
Shows the segfault occurs immediately after test_datetime.py begins running, following the successful completion of test__exceptions.py
Confirms pytest was able to run and complete one test module successfully before crashing in the next one
Case identifier explicitly references a segmentation fault issue, confirming this is the expected failure mode being investigated

suggested_fix: Unable to determine from context; the segmentation fault requires debugging with a stack trace, gdb output, or valgrind report. Verify that NumPy C extensions (especially datetime-related code) were correctly compiled without memory safety issues.

model_info._backfill_note: Pre-Codex-2026-05-14 F2 fix. The Claude shim now persists _model_info from every API call; this row predates that emit so resolved_model and usage are not recoverable. requested_model is set to the shim-level alias (haiku for v1, sonnet for v2) which is what the post-fix shim emits.
